t1.name: Microplastics Sources and Origins
t1.description: Identification and characterization of primary and secondary sources of microplastics entering marine environments, including manufacturing, degradation, and human activities.

t1.1.name: Primary Microplastics Production
t1.1.description: Microplastics intentionally manufactured at small sizes for industrial and consumer applications including cosmetics, personal care products, and microbeads.

t1.2.name: Secondary Microplastics Formation
t1.2.description: Formation of microplastics through environmental degradation of larger plastic debris, including weathering, mechanical breakdown, and photodegradation processes.

t1.3.name: Terrestrial and Atmospheric Transport
t1.3.description: Pathways through which microplastics reach marine environments from land-based sources including wastewater discharge, stormwater runoff, atmospheric deposition, and riverine transport.

t2.name: Microplastics Distribution and Dynamics
t2.description: Spatial distribution patterns, abundance, and movement of microplastics throughout marine ecosystems including water column, sediments, and coastal zones.

t2.1.name: Open Ocean and Deep-Sea Distribution
t2.1.description: Occurrence and accumulation of microplastics in offshore waters, oceanic gyres, and deep-sea environments including benthic sediments.

t2.2.name: Coastal and Estuarine Hotspots
t2.2.description: Elevated microplastic concentrations in nearshore zones, beaches, estuaries, and tidal areas where terrestrial inputs converge with marine systems.

t2.3.name: Biotic Uptake and Trophic Transfer
t2.3.description: Ingestion of microplastics by marine organisms and movement through food webs from primary producers to apex predators.

t3.name: Ecological and Toxicological Impacts
t3.description: Effects of microplastic exposure on marine organisms and ecosystems, including physical harm, chemical toxicity, and ecosystem-level consequences.

t3.1.name: Organismal Toxicity and Physiological Effects
t3.1.description: Direct toxic effects on marine organisms including oxidative stress, endocrine disruption, reproductive impairment, and physical obstruction.

t3.2.name: Chemical Contaminant Sorption and Transfer
t3.2.description: Role of microplastics as vectors for persistent organic pollutants, heavy metals, and additives, and their transfer to biota upon ingestion.
